title: Message Envelopes, Binary Bodies, Schemas, and Evolution · Contract Lens: Runtime Schema Validation
summary: Consumers parse Buffer content directly into trusted domain types, overload routing keys with identity data, lack stable event IDs and versions, and break when producers add or remove fields. Planned artifact: Add and verify the runtime schema validation increment in versioned message envelope and compatibility matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate unknown envelopes and payloads with strict runtime schemas, bounded collections, semantic refinements, and explicit rejection evidence.
Define message ID, type, schema version, source, subject, occurrence time, correlation, causation, tenant, and trace context without conflating them.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Runtime Schema Validation · Theory Runtime Schema Validation
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 8225 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound bytes and validate content type, identity, and schema version before parsing effects; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then add one subscriber responsibility without changing publisher intent. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Runtime Schema Validation contract for the supplied data.
